system.description:
CLASS DICE: D10

Gifts of the Fair

You can add your D10 to your dice pool any time you're assisted by an activated Gift.

Adventurers love gold. With their propensity for taking anything that isn't nailed down makes them all thieves... which makes the classical fantasy prejudice against rogues a little odd. They're all at it. Why do rogues get the glances?

But anyone who's ever met one knows why people are suspicious about the Neo.

There's a special kind of gold, and the Neo's magical technology needs to be activated by this every day. If they can't find enough then all their gifts mean nothing.

Adventurers all want gold but no-one needs it like a Neo.

Play a Neo if you want to...

  • Be the chromed-up cyberpunk rogue in the fantasy adventuring party.
  • Select from an armoury of magic-cybernetic gifts you can personalise to your heart's content.
  • Hunt desperately for the next score to fuel your powers.
system.coreNature:
Adventurers love gold. With their propensity for taking anything that isn't nailed down makes them all thieves... which makes the classical fantasy prejudice against rogues a little odd. They're all at it. Why do rogues get the glances?

But anyone who's ever met one knows why people are suspicious about the Neo.

There's a special kind of gold, and the Neo's magical technology needs to be activated by this every day. If they can't find enough then all their gifts mean nothing. They can't skip between shadows, dance through the sky or punch a hole through a Fallen's head from half a mile away.

Adventurers all want gold but no-one needs it like a Neo.

PLAY A NEO IF YOU WANT TO...

  • Be the chromed-up cyberpunk rogue in the fantasy adventuring party.
  • Select from an armoury of magic-cybernetic gifts you can personalise to your heart's content.
  • Hunt desperately for the next score to fuel your powers.

coreAbility.name: GIFTS OF THE FAIR
coreAbility.description: You can add your D10 to your dice pool any time you're assisted by an activated Gift.

looks.name: Black leather, studs and chrome, white leather, bleach and catsuits
Billowing black cloak and sinister scarlet eyes
Exposed metallic exoskeleton and vat-grown muscle

fields.label: Fair Gold
fields.description:
Fair Gold is the techno-magical fuel which powers your gifts. It takes the form of small coins, most commonly found in the corpses of Fallen. Your AI will tell you if any Fair Gold is nearby.

A single piece of Fair Gold will activate one of your gifts (and its upgrades) until the following Dawn.
fields.hint: Track how many Fair Gold you possess. They disappear at dawn.

fields.label: Neotech
fields.initial:
"

You always have an AI system and basic ability to access the Fair Field—an invisible technological data network. Your AI system is capable of talking with you and performing basic tasks relating to your gifts and upgrades.

To use your Neotech to hack Fallen, computers, automated systems and anything technological, roll a dice pool based on your Int. Each success lets you bypass a particular problem your target is causing.

Special: actively subvert the system to perform one action.

"

fields.label: Overcharge
fields.initial:
"

You can use extra Fair Gold to simulate an upgrade in a one-off fashion. To overcharge an activated gift, describe your desired effect. The GM sets a cost (in gold) to achieve this effect.

Spend the gold, and roll your D10:

  • If the result is odd, it works perfectly.
  • If it's even, it mostly works but something goes amiss.

One gold is enough to temporarily remove a Gift's limitations, extend its capacities or ape an Upgrade.

Two gold is enough to improve the gift in several ways.

Three or more gold let you perform truly spectacular feats.

"

fields.label: Gifts of the Fair
fields.description: Select one technomagical gift when creating your Neo, and one of its upgrades. You can only take a single upgrade marked Defensive unless an advancement says otherwise.

fields.label: New Gift
fields.description: You acquire another gift of the Fair. How did you get it? Choose one: gain a new Gift, or Upgrade an existing Gift.

fields.label: Upgrade
fields.description: You're getting better at harnessing your gifts. Pick an extra upgrade on one of them.

fields.label: Gold Source
fields.description: At the start of each day, you receive one Fair Gold via a reasonably safe method. What is it?

fields.label: Sick-Ass Ride
fields.description: You can create, summon or otherwise generate a vehicle that can transport the rest of the party, and a couple of friends. When in this form, you travel as fast as a horse.
fields: {"key":"rideAppearance","type":"select","label":"What does it look like?","choices":["1950s sci-fi, all chrome","2010s heavy-duty serious tech","2070s bleeding-edge cyberpunk","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

{"key":"rideOrigin","type":"select","label":"Where does it come from?","choices":["You summon it","It emerges from your body","You transform into it","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

{"key":"rideUpgrades","type":"multiSelect","label":"Ride Upgrades","choices":["It can travel underwater","It can travel in a vacuum","It can carry another party's worth of people","The engine is silent","It can reach orbit","It can fly (double speed each time taken)"],"initial":[],"hint":"Pick one when you take this advancement. Any time you choose an upgrade, you can upgrade your ride instead.","description":""}

fields.label: Pawn Shop
fields.description: The Neo has an extradimensional contact who has an interest in certain objects. They've agreed to buy them in exchange for Fair Gold.
fields: {"key":"pawnContact","type":"select","label":"Who is this contact?","choices":["A Single Fair, turned eccentric from the loss of their other half","A Demon Prince with a crooked halo","A time-traveller in some manner of public convenience","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

{"key":"pawnWants","type":"select","label":"What do they want?","choices":["An exquisite meal they've never ate before","A secret that would ruin someone's life","Skulls of unusual species, but only once for any single species","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

fields.label: The Big Score
fields.description: You have become aware of how to rip off an entity for Fair Gold. You gain 2 gold every morning as long as you keep up the con or racket. Once a session, the GM can tweak the plot to show the difficulties of maintaining the enterprise.
fields: {"key":"theCon","type":"select","label":"What's the con?","choices":["Skimming Fair Gold from the local Neo Guild","Convinced a cult their god wants Fair Gold offerings","Found the back door to a Reality Mine","Running a fake protection scheme (Mayfly Dragon)","Running a Pyramid scheme (eternal-life-guaranteed)","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

fields.label: Hackromancer
fields.description: The Neo gains the ability to create a beacon that gathers Fallen. When the beacon is activated with a piece of Fair Gold, it is as if the heavens opened and a biblical flood of Fallen fell upon the land. You do not control these Fallen.

fields.label: Deus Ex Machina
fields.description: You have gained access to a god-machine, capable of miraculous feats. It can do one impossible thing, but not before it's fuelled by an impossibly rare material. When activated, it will achieve what it does, and then consuming its fuel and shut down.
fields: {"key":"machineForm","type":"select","label":"What form is it?","choices":["A deserted, confused spaceship","A Mecha suit missing a limb","A naive pet AI-God","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

{"key":"machinePower","type":"select","label":"What powers it?","choices":["Fair Gold purified by a difficult, obscure method","A bottled aspect of a god","The possibility of one of your dreams ever being real","Something else"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}

fields.label: Chosen of the Fair
fields.description: You gain a special relationship with the Fair, and are able to come to an understanding with them—at least, as much as anyone can understand the Fair. Reselect your gifts and upgrades in a burst of apotheosis. You gain the ability to summon the Fair once a session with a strange ritual.

fields.label: An Interesting Decision (Level 10)
fields.description: You are confronted by two of the Fair. They explain that you're a creature of both worlds, perfectly balanced between the two. They offer you a gift with uncertain consequences.
fields: {"key":"acceptedGift","type":"boolean","label":"Accepted the gift?","initial":false,"hint":"If accepted, gain a gift with an upgrade. Either way, gain ability to have a second Defensive upgrade.","description":""}
